description:
Holy water bucket

TYPE: Holy water bucket
AUTHOR: Venetian workshop
DATE: 15th or 16th century
MATERIAL AND TECHNIQUE: Embossed copper and matt enamel paint
MEASUREMENTS: Height 14 cm, diameter at the base 11 cm.

Base: glazed blue with gold scale motifs. Cup: glazed blue and white with golden vegetable motifs.

Title: The Shepherd Boy's Rest
Sculpture

TYPE: Sculpture
AUTHOR: Raffaele Beliazzi
DATE: 1879
MATERIAL AND TECHNIQUE: Marble
MEASURES: 60x140x45 cm

Shows a shepherd boy resting, depicting social commentary on child exploitation. Signed by the artist and part of the picture gallery since 1917.

Plates

TYPE: Plates
AUTHOR: Manufacture of Sèvres
DATE: uncertain between the second half of the 18th century and the first half of the 19th century.
MATERIAL AND TECHNIQUE: Gilded bronze and porcelain.
MEASUREMENTS: Diameter of 25 cm.

Decorative plates with portraits of Louis XVI and Marie Antoinette. Likely a copy from the Bourbon restoration era.

Musical instrument - violin

TYPE: Musical instrument - violin
AUTHOR: Giuseppe Odoardi known as Villan d'Ascoli
DATE: second half of the 18th century
MATERIAL AND TECHNIQUE: Carved wood and varnishing
MEASUREMENTS: 25.5x14.8 cm.

Stradivarian violin with unique features such as carved curl and F-shaped slits on the sides of the bridge.

Reliquary

TYPE: Reliquary
AUTHOR: Ascoli area
DATE: about XV century
MATERIAL AND TECHNIQUE: Golden copper and enamels

Linked to Ascoli's goldsmith production, with engraved plant motifs and a statuette of Saint Catherine of Alexandria.

Title: Amorous Walk
Painting by Giuseppe Pelizza da Volpedo

TYPE: Painting by Giuseppe Pelizza da Volpedo
DATE: around 1901
MATERIAL AND TECHNIQUE: Oil on Canvas
MEASUREMENTS: diameter of 100 cm

Part of the Idilli project, depicting an amorous scene in the nature with pointillist technique and chiaroscuro.

Title: Portrait
Painting by Antonio Mancini

TYPE: Painting by Antonio Mancini
DATE: first quarter of the 20th century
MATERIAL AND TECHNIQUE: Oil on Canvas
MEASUREMENTS: 45x43 cm

Known for dense and material color, often mixing materials like mirrors and confetti.

Title: Annunciation Painting by Guido Reni TYPE: Painting by Guido Reni DATE: around 1628-1629 MATERIAL AND TECHNIQUE: Oil on Canvas MEASUREMENTS: 237x154 cm Commissioned for the altar of the chapel in the church of 'Santa Maria della Carità'. Represents the Annunciation with Mary and the angel, notable for its composition and lighting. Artist Guido Reni
(Bologna 1574 - 1642)

One of the greatest painters of seventeenth-century classicism. Studied under the Carraccis and gained fame for works like 'Atalanta and Ippomene'. His style evolved from mannerism to personal expression, seen in works like 'Madonna del Rosario'. Died in Bologna in 1642.

Title: Saint Francis receives the stigmata Painting by Tiziano Vecellio TYPE: Painting by Tiziano Vecellio DATE: around 1561 MATERIAL AND TECHNIQUE: Oil on Canvas MEASUREMENTS: 298x177 cm Commissioned by Desiderio Guidoni for the chapel of the Guidoni family. Depicts the stigmata of St. Francis, with luminous rays from Christ. Noted for its use of light and color. Artist Tiziano Vecellio
(Pieve di Cadore 1488/1490 - Venice 1576)

Unanimously recognized as one of the greatest painters in history. A versatile artist, he excelled in sacred, mythological, and portrait painting. Renowned for his innovation and entrepreneurial spirit, he transformed Venetian painting and influenced artists like Tintoretto and Veronese.
